content: Inference-speed differentiators: the KERNEL matters more than the quant FORMAT. Marlin (IST-DASLB) is the optimized 4-bit W4A16 kernel for NVIDIA Ampere/Hopper, fuses dequant+matmul; AWQ-MARLIN hits 741 tok/s on H200 vs 461 raw (1.61x), GPTQ-MARLIN 712 tok/s. Same AWQ weights run 68 vs 741 tok/s depending on kernel. GGUF is CPU/edge-optimized (SIMD), slow in vLLM (93 tok/s, unpack overhead). Speculative decoding (draft model or EAGLE feature-level head) multiplies tokens/forward pass; stacks cleanly with quantization. EXL2 fastest single-user on NVIDIA via ExLlamaV2 (52-56 tok/s RTX3090 13B). For CPU-only pure-Python engine like Spectral-Stream, these GPU kernels are NOT applicable — its speed edge is none; honesty/transparency is the only viable differentiator.
